data.prompt:
- Title and subtitle
  - Title: Bricks and Black Sails
  - Subtitle: A Pirate Odyssey Built One Stud at a Time
- Cover concept overview
  - Big, imposing LEGO-built galleon dominates the frame, angled three-quarters toward the viewer, bow lunging out of a brick-built wave
  - Scale and menace emphasized by towering masts made from stacked round bricks, ragged black sails tiled from matte plates
  - Horizon low; sky is a gradient of studded plates punctuated by brick-built storm clouds and lightning made from translucent rods
  - Gold-studded treasure surfacing in the wake; a shattered brick pier in the foreground for depth
  - LEGO brick texture is visible everywhere: studs, seams, and part geometry must read clearly
- Central ship details
  - Hull: Layered plates and curved slopes in dark brown and black, with visible stud lines; reinforced keel using inverted slopes
  - Figurehead: Skull assembled from white curved slopes and tiles, eye sockets from round 1x1 studs
  - Sails: Patchwork matte-black tiles and plates, torn edges suggested via staggered plates; crossbones insignia tiled from white plates
  - Rigging: Bar elements and clip pieces; lanterns from trans-yellow round studs
  - Deck: Cannon ports with brick-built cannons; minifig silhouettes only as scale, no character focus
- Water and environment
  - Ocean: Mosaic of dark teal, azure, and trans-blue tiles with foam crests from white cheese slopes
  - Spray: Trans-clear studs bursting around the bow; motion lines suggested with staggered plate offsets
  - Sky: Moody storm gradient from gunmetal to indigo; brick-built lightning forks
  - Lighting: Low, dramatic rim light from starboard; subtle volumetric fog over the horizon
- Mood and genre signal
  - Tone: Epic, dangerous, adventurous
  - Age target: 10+ to adult crossover
  - Visual cue: Cinematic, photoreal LEGO build, not cartoonish
- Color palette
  - Primary: Black, dark brown, gunmetal, deep indigo
  - Accents: Trans-yellow, white, dark teal, gold
  - Type color: Antique gold or bone white over darker areas for contrast
- Typography
  - Title: Tall, chiseled display face with subtle bevel effect (e.g., Cinzel, Trajan-style, or custom blocky engraved lettering)
  - Subtitle: Condensed sans (e.g., Bebas Neue, League Gothic) in bone white
  - Author name: Small caps serif in antique gold
  - Effects: Light emboss, soft drop shadow for legibility over textured bricks
  - Placement: Title arched along the top third; subtitle centered below; author at bottom
- Layout and print specs
  - Trim size: 6 x 9 inches (152 x 229 mm)
  - Bleed: 0.125 in (3 mm) on all sides
  - Safe margin: 0.25 in (6 mm)
  - Spine: 0.8 in for ~300 pages on 55 lb paper (adjust to final page count)
  - Back cover: Blurb top-left; review quotes area; barcode 2 x 1.2 in bottom-right; background continues the studded sky
- Back cover blurb
  - When a storm tears the horizon and the sea rises like stacked stone, the Night Bastion answers with iron and thunder. Built from the bones of a hundred voyages, her crew hunts a myth: a map scattered across the ocean in gilded bricks. Every port hides a piece. Every foe wants the last. And the sea keeps the cruelest dues.
- Spine elements
  - Miniature skull-and-sabers brick icon at the top
  - Title in stacked small caps
  - Author surname centered
  - Publisher mark at the base
- Composition guide
  - Rule of thirds: Ship's prow at upper-left intersection; skull figurehead near center-left
  - Depth: Foreground wreckage, midground ship, background storm; atmospheric perspective with desaturation in the distance
  - Balance: Heavy mass of ship offset by angular lightning and cresting wave
- Rendering style options
  - Photoreal 3D render: Ray-traced plastics, subtle micro-scratches, fingerprints, and specular anisotropy on studs
  - High-detail illustration: Painterly but with accurate brick geometry and part IDs
- Alt text prompt for image generation
  - A colossal pirate galleon made entirely from LEGO bricks charges through stormy brick-built seas under a thunderous, studded sky. Black tiled sails with a white tile skull-and-crossbones billow from mast assemblies, the bow cutting a foamy mosaic of trans-blue and white pieces. The scene is cinematic, dark, and epic, with dramatic rim lighting and visible brick textures.
- Production prompts (use for CGI or AI tools)
  - Photoreal CGI prompt: Ultra-detailed LEGO pirate ship, three-masted galleon, all elements made from LEGO bricks and plates; black patchwork tiled sails with white tile skull-and-crossbones; dark brown and black hull with visible studs and seams; trans-blue ocean made from tiles and plates with white cheese-slope foam; stormy sky built from studded plates, brick-built lightning; cinematic rim light from starboard; shallow depth of field; 8k, ray-traced plastics, micro-scratches, realistic reflections
  - Illustration prompt: Epic LEGO brick pirate ship in a storm, three-quarter view, towering masts, ragged black tiled sails, skull figurehead, brick ocean waves, lightning in studded sky, high contrast, textured brushwork, accurate LEGO geometry
- Variant treatments
  - Dark edition: Heavier vignetting, colder steel-blue palette, glossier plastics
  - Adventure edition: Warmer sunset rim light with trans-orange highlights on edges
  - Kids edition: Slightly brighter hues, larger title type, add map fragments as floating brick icons
- Logo and icon ideas
  - Brick skull badge: 3x3 tile skull with 1x1 round eyes and crossed 1x4 plates
  - Compass rose built from wedge plates in gold and bone white
- File delivery checklist
  - Front, spine, back combined PDF/X-1a with bleed
  - Separate high-res front cover JPEG (300 dpi) for digital stores
  - Fonts outlined or embedded; color in CMYK for print, RGB variant for ebook
- Licensing notes
  - Ensure all imagery clearly depicts physical LEGO-style bricks; avoid using official brand logos
  - If photographing real builds, confirm you own or license the model and photography
- Quick tagline options
  - The sea is built to break you
  - Fortune favors the bold and the brick
  - Where the waves click into place
data.output:
## Book Cover Design Brief: Bricks and Black Sails

---

### **Project Title:** Bricks and Black Sails
### **Subtitle:** A Pirate Odyssey Built One Stud at a Time

---

### **1. Overview & Core Concept**

This cover needs to convey an epic, dangerous, and adventurous tone, targeting a 10+ to adult crossover audience. The visual style should be cinematic and photoreal LEGO, emphasizing the intricate construction of bricks, not a cartoonish depiction. The central image will be a powerful, imposing LEGO-built galleon dominating a stormy, brick-built sea.

---

### **2. Front Cover Elements**

**A. Central Ship Details:**
*   **Main Subject:** A big, imposing LEGO-built galleon dominates the frame, angled three-quarters toward the viewer, bow lunging out of a brick-built wave.
*   **Scale & Menace:** Emphasized by towering masts made from stacked round bricks, and ragged black sails tiled from matte plates. Minifig silhouettes can be used on deck purely for scale, no character focus.
*   **Hull:** Layered plates and curved slopes in dark brown and black, with visible stud lines. Reinforced keel using inverted slopes is crucial for realism.
*   **Figurehead:** A menacing skull assembled from white curved slopes and tiles, with eye sockets formed from round 1x1 studs.
*   **Sails:** Patchwork matte-black tiles and plates, with torn edges suggested via staggered plate arrangements. A prominent crossbones insignia tiled from white plates on the main sail.
*   **Rigging:** Detailed using bar elements and clip pieces. Lanterns on the rigging should be represented by trans-yellow round studs.
*   **Deck:** Clearly show cannon ports with brick-built cannons.

**B. Water and Environment:**
*   **Ocean:** A dynamic mosaic of dark teal, azure, and trans-blue tiles and plates. Foam crests should be depicted using white cheese slopes.
*   **Spray:** Trans-clear studs bursting dramatically around the bow of the ship. Motion lines can be suggested with staggered plate offsets in the water.
*   **Sky:** A moody, studded plate gradient, transitioning from gunmetal to deep indigo. Punctuated by brick-built storm clouds and jagged lightning forks made from translucent rods.
*   **Horizon:** Kept low to emphasize the scale of the ship and the vastness of the storm.
*   **Foreground:** A shattered brick pier or wreckage in the immediate foreground to add depth and context.
*   **Treasure:** Gold-studded treasure should be surfacing in the ship's wake, hinting at the pursuit.
*   **Lighting:** Dramatic low, hard rim light from the starboard side (viewer's right). Subtle volumetric fog over the horizon to enhance atmospheric perspective.

**C. Mood and Genre Signal:**
*   **Tone:** Epic, dangerous, adventurous.
*   **Age Target:** 10+ to adult crossover.
*   **Visual Cue:** Cinematic, photoreal LEGO build, not cartoonish. The LEGO brick texture, studs, seams, and part geometry must be clearly visible everywhere.

**D. Color Palette:**
*   **Primary:** Black, dark brown, gunmetal, deep indigo.
*   **Accents:** Trans-yellow, white, dark teal, gold (for treasure and highlights).

**E. Typography:**
*   **Title (`Bricks and Black Sails`):** Tall, chiseled display face with a subtle bevel effect (e.g., Cinzel, Trajan-style, or custom blocky engraved lettering). Type color: Antique gold or bone white for high contrast over darker areas. Placement: Arched gently along the top third of the cover.
*   **Subtitle (`A Pirate Odyssey Built One Stud at a Time`):** Condensed sans-serif (e.g., Bebas Neue, League Gothic) in bone white. Placement: Centered directly below the title.
*   **Author Name:** Small caps serif in antique gold. Placement: At the bottom of the cover.
*   **Effects:** Light emboss and a soft drop shadow on all typography for legibility over the textured brick background.

**F. Composition Guide:**
*   **Rule of Thirds:** The ship's prow should be positioned near the upper-left intersection point. The skull figurehead should be near the center-left intersection.
*   **Depth:** Strong visual depth created by foreground wreckage, the midground ship, and the background storm. Atmospheric perspective should be subtly applied with slight desaturation in the distance.
*   **Balance:** The heavy mass of the ship should be dynamically offset by angular lightning strikes and the cresting wave.

**G. Rendering Style Options:**
*   **Photoreal 3D Render (Preferred):** Emphasis on ray-traced plastics, subtle micro-scratches, fingerprints, and realistic specular anisotropy on individual studs and plates.
*   **High-Detail Illustration:** Painterly, but with absolute accuracy in brick geometry and visible part IDs where appropriate, maintaining a sense of tangible construction.

**H. Alt Text & Production Prompts (for CGI or AI tools):**
*   **Alt Text Prompt:** A colossal pirate galleon made entirely from LEGO bricks charges through stormy brick-built seas under a thunderous, studded sky. Black tiled sails with a white tile skull-and-crossbones billow from mast assemblies, the bow cutting a foamy mosaic of trans-blue and white pieces. The scene is cinematic, dark, and epic, with dramatic rim lighting and visible brick textures.
*   **Photoreal CGI Prompt:** Ultra-detailed LEGO pirate ship, three-masted galleon, all elements made from LEGO bricks and plates; black patchwork tiled sails with white tile skull-and-crossbones; dark brown and black hull with visible studs and seams; trans-blue ocean made from tiles and plates with white cheese-slope foam; stormy sky built from studded plates, brick-built lightning; cinematic rim light from starboard; shallow depth of field; 8k, ray-traced plastics, micro-scratches, realistic reflections, subtle fingerprints.
*   **Illustration Prompt:** Epic LEGO brick pirate ship in a storm, three-quarter view, towering masts, ragged black tiled sails, skull figurehead, brick ocean waves, lightning in studded sky, high contrast, textured brushwork, accurate LEGO geometry, dark fantasy art style.

**I. Variant Treatments (Optional, for future editions/marketing):**
*   **Dark Edition:** Heavier vignetting, colder steel-blue palette, glossier plastics.
*   **Adventure Edition:** Warmer sunset rim light with trans-orange highlights on edges.
*   **Kids Edition:** Slightly brighter hues, larger title type, add map fragments as floating brick icons.

**J. Logo and Icon Ideas (for branding/merchandise):**
*   **Brick Skull Badge:** A simple 3x3 tile skull with 1x1 round eyes and crossed 1x4 plates in white.
*   **Compass Rose:** Built from wedge plates in gold and bone white.

---

### **3. Back Cover Elements**

**A. Back Cover Blurb:**
"When a storm tears the horizon and the sea rises like stacked stone, the Night Bastion answers with iron and thunder. Built from the bones of a hundred voyages, her crew hunts a myth: a map scattered across the ocean in gilded bricks. Every port hides a piece. Every foe wants the last. And the sea keeps the cruelest dues."

**B. Layout:**
*   **Background:** The studded sky gradient from the front cover should continue across the back.
*   **Blurb Placement:** Top-left area, allowing space for other elements.
*   **Review Quotes Area:** Designated space for potential review quotes.
*   **Barcode:** Standard 2 x 1.2 inches (51 x 30 mm) placement, bottom-right.

---

### **4. Spine Elements**

*   **Top:** Miniature skull-and-sabers brick icon.
*   **Middle:** Title in stacked small caps (`BRICKS AND BLACK SAILS`).
*   **Below Title:** Author surname centered.
*   **Base:** Publisher mark/logo.

---

### **5. Print & File Specifications**

*   **Trim Size:** 6 x 9 inches (152 x 229 mm).
*   **Bleed:** 0.125 inches (3 mm) on all sides.
*   **Safe Margin:** 0.25 inches (6 mm) from trim on all internal elements.
*   **Spine Width:** 0.8 inches (approx. for 300 pages on 55 lb paper; confirm exact width with final page count).

**File Delivery Checklist:**
*   Front, spine, and back combined PDF/X-1a with bleed.
*   Separate high-resolution front cover JPEG (300 dpi) for digital stores.
*   All fonts outlined or embedded.
*   Color mode: CMYK for print, RGB variant for ebook/digital use.

---

### **6. Licensing Notes**

*   Ensure all imagery clearly depicts physical LEGO-style bricks and construction.
*   Explicitly avoid using any official LEGO brand logos, minifigure designs, or copyrighted intellectual property. The aesthetic is "LEGO-inspired," not "official LEGO product."
*   If photographing real builds, confirm ownership or appropriate licensing for the model and photography rights.

---

### **7. Quick Tagline Options (for marketing use)**

*   The sea is built to break you.
*   Fortune favors the bold and the brick.
*   Where the waves click into place.

data.prompt: Generate a book preview / mockup with the input image as the cover

Advanced Workflow:

This example mimics a professional design pipeline.

1. Input: A highly detailed specification for a book cover.
2. Step 1: Generates the raw vertical book cover art.
3. Step 2: Takes the generated cover and creates wide-format marketing mockups.
